stimulus: Generations of science fiction ______blank have drawn inspiration from the visionary stories of celebrated British author H. G. Wells (1866–1946).
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between a subject and its verb. No punctuation is needed between the subject "authors" and the verb phrase "have drawn." Separating a subject from its verb with punctuation results in an ungrammatical sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the subject and its verb. Choice B is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the subject and its verb. Choice D is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the subject and its verb.
